Business Administration.title: Business Finance
Business Administration.description: Fundamental principles of corporate finance including financial analysis, capital budgeting, and financing decisions. Students learn to evaluate investment opportunities, manage working capital, and understand the relationship between risk and return in business contexts.
learning_objectives: Analyze financial statements and calculate key financial ratios
Evaluate capital budgeting decisions using NPV and IRR
Understand cost of capital and capital structure decisions
Assess working capital management strategies
Apply risk and return concepts to financial decision-making
covered_materials: Time value of money and present value calculations
Financial statement analysis and ratio analysis
Capital budgeting techniques and decision criteria
Cost of capital and weighted average cost of capital
Capital structure and dividend policy
Working capital management
Risk assessment and portfolio theory
International financial management basics

Business Administration.title: Operations Management
Business Administration.description: Study of operations and production management including process design, capacity planning, inventory management, and quality control. Students learn to optimize operational efficiency and effectiveness in manufacturing and service organizations.
learning_objectives: Design efficient operational processes and workflows
Apply inventory management and control techniques
Implement quality management and continuous improvement practices
Analyze capacity planning and scheduling decisions
Evaluate supply chain management strategies
covered_materials: Operations strategy and competitive advantage
Process analysis and design
Capacity planning and facility location
Inventory management models
Quality management and Six Sigma
Project management and scheduling
Supply chain management fundamentals
Lean operations and continuous improvement

Business Administration.title: Human Resource Management
Business Administration.description: Comprehensive study of human resource management practices including recruitment, selection, training, performance management, and employee relations. Students learn to manage human capital effectively while understanding legal and ethical considerations in HR.
learning_objectives: Develop effective recruitment and selection strategies
Design training and development programs
Implement performance management systems
Understand employment law and compliance requirements
Apply compensation and benefits management principles
covered_materials: HR planning and job analysis
Recruitment and selection processes
Training and development strategies
Performance appraisal and management
Compensation and benefits administration
Employee relations and labor law
Diversity and inclusion in the workplace
HR information systems and analytics

Game Design Media Courses.title: Game Theory and Mechanics
Game Design Media Courses.description: Study of game design theory, mechanics, and player psychology including game balance, progression systems, and player motivation. Students learn to design engaging gameplay experiences while understanding the psychological and social aspects of gaming.
learning_objectives: Understand fundamental game design theory and principles
Analyze game mechanics and their impact on player experience
Design balanced and engaging gameplay systems
Apply player psychology to game design decisions
Evaluate games critically from a design perspective
covered_materials: Game design theory and frameworks
Core mechanics and game systems
Player motivation and engagement
Game balance and difficulty curves
Progression systems and rewards
Social dynamics in gaming
Game genre analysis and conventions
Playtesting and iteration methods

Game Design Media Courses.title: 3D Game Development
Game Design Media Courses.description: Advanced 3D game development including 3D graphics programming, physics simulation, and complex game systems. Students create sophisticated 3D games while learning advanced technical and design concepts.
learning_objectives: Develop complete 3D games using modern engines
Implement 3D graphics and rendering techniques
Create complex 3D gameplay mechanics
Optimize 3D games for performance
Apply advanced game development patterns
covered_materials: 3D game engines and development environments
3D graphics programming and shaders
Physics simulation and collision detection
Camera systems and player controls
3D user interface design
Performance optimization techniques
Advanced lighting and visual effects
Platform deployment and distribution

Game Design and Media.title: Introduction to Game Design
Game Design and Media.description: Foundational course covering game design vocabulary, analytical frameworks, and prototyping skills. Students explore what constitutes a game, study the MDA framework, examine player psychology and motivation, and learn to build and playtest paper prototypes while documenting their designs in a game design document.
learning_objectives: Apply the MDA (Mechanics, Dynamics, Aesthetics) framework to analyze and design games
Explain player psychology concepts including flow theory and Bartle's taxonomy
Build and iterate on paper prototypes through structured playtesting
Classify games by genre and evaluate genre-blending as a design strategy
Create a comprehensive game design document for an original concept
covered_materials: Game definitions and core elements (rules, goals, feedback, voluntary participation)
MDA framework and fundamental design principles
Player psychology, motivation, and flow theory
Game genres and genre conventions
Paper prototyping and rapid iteration techniques
Playtesting methods and data collection
Game design document structure and purpose

Game Design and Media.title: Game Theory and Mechanics
Game Design and Media.description: Examines the formal systems that make games work, from core mechanics and feedback loops to game balance and reward systems. Students analyze published games through a systems lens, design balanced mechanical interactions, and build original tabletop games that produce emergent gameplay from minimal rules.
learning_objectives: Identify and analyze core mechanics, secondary mechanics, and their relationships
Distinguish between positive and negative feedback loops and predict their impact on pacing
Design balanced game systems using symmetric, asymmetric, and intransitive relationships
Apply probability and reward schedule theory to game reward design
Create rule sets that produce emergent gameplay from simple mechanical interactions
covered_materials: Core mechanics and the atom of gameplay
Positive and negative feedback loops
Game balance: symmetry, asymmetry, and fairness
Reward systems, probability, and expected value
Progression systems and difficulty curves
Emergent gameplay and systems thinking
Ethical implications of variable-ratio reward schedules

Game Design and Media.title: Digital Art Fundamentals
Game Design and Media.description: Teaches the visual principles and technical skills needed for game art production, covering color theory, composition, digital drawing, pixel art, vector graphics, and typography. Students create game-ready assets including character sprites, environment tiles, UI elements, and texture atlases while building a cohesive art style guide.
learning_objectives: Apply color theory and visual composition principles to digital game art
Create readable pixel art sprites and animations at common game resolutions
Design scalable vector game assets and apply typographic principles to game UI
Build a professional asset pipeline from source files to engine-ready exports
Develop a cohesive art style guide for an original game concept
covered_materials: Color theory for digital media (RGB, HSB, color harmonies)
Composition, visual hierarchy, and negative space
Digital drawing tools, layers, and blend modes
Pixel art techniques: dithering, anti-aliasing, and sprite animation
Vector graphics, Bezier curves, and boolean operations
Typography fundamentals and font pairing for games
UI design basics, sprite sheets, and texture atlases

Game Design and Media.title: 2D Game Development
Game Design and Media.description: Hands-on course in building complete 2D games using Godot 4, covering sprite animation, tile maps, physics systems, camera work, and save/load architecture. Students implement polished platformer mechanics with coyote time and input buffering, create parallax backgrounds, design particle effects, and deliver a playable vertical slice with enemy AI.
learning_objectives: Set up and structure 2D game projects with proper scene tree architecture
Implement character movement with animation state machines and game feel polish
Design tile-based levels with autotiling, collision layers, and one-way platforms
Build camera systems with parallax scrolling and screen shake effects
Create particle effects and implement a versioned save/load system
covered_materials: 2D game engine comparison and Godot 4 project setup
Sprite animation and finite state machines
TileMap configuration, autotiling, and level design
2D physics, collision systems, and game feel tuning
Camera systems, parallax scrolling, and look-ahead
GPU particle systems and visual effects
Save/load architecture and data serialization

Game Design and Media.title: 3D Modeling and Animation
Game Design and Media.description: Comprehensive course in creating game-ready 3D assets using Blender, from polygon modeling and UV unwrapping through PBR texturing, rigging, and skeletal animation. Students produce characters with clean quad topology, full PBR material sets, functional armatures with IK chains, and animated walk cycles exported in glTF format.
learning_objectives: Model 3D assets with clean quad-dominant topology suitable for animation
UV unwrap meshes with hidden seams and consistent texel density
Create complete PBR texture sets including normal map baking from high-poly sculpts
Rig characters with armatures, IK chains, and weight-painted deformation
Animate walk cycles and export animations in game-engine-compatible formats
covered_materials: Blender interface, viewport navigation, and polygon mesh fundamentals
Box modeling, edge loops, and quad-dominant topology
UV unwrapping, seam placement, and texel density
PBR texturing, material creation, and normal map baking
Rigging with armatures, IK/FK, and bone constraints
Skeletal animation, walk cycles, and key pose techniques
glTF export and NLA animation organization

Game Design and Media.title: Interactive Storytelling
Game Design and Media.description: Explores the craft of building narratives that respond to player choice, covering branching structures, dialogue systems, environmental storytelling, and character arc design. Students implement interactive narratives in Twine and Ink, design quest chains with meaningful consequences, and produce a capstone interactive narrative experience.
learning_objectives: Map and evaluate branching narrative architectures including foldback and quality-based structures
Design dialogue systems and write character-driven branching conversations
Apply environmental storytelling techniques to convey narrative without text
Create character arcs that function within player-agency-driven narrative structures
Structure quests that serve both gameplay and narrative goals simultaneously
covered_materials: Interactive narrative foundations and player agency
Branching narrative structures and combinatorial complexity management
Dialogue systems, conversation design, and Ink scripting
Environmental storytelling and spatial narrative
Character arcs, defined protagonists, and blank-slate avatars
Quest design, dependency graphs, and narrative middleware
Localization considerations for interactive content

Game Design and Media.title: Game Programming
Game Design and Media.description: Advanced game programming covering architecture patterns, Entity-Component-System design, AI pathfinding, spatial partitioning, networking, and shader programming. Students build a working 2D game engine in C++ with ECS architecture, implement A* pathfinding with spatial partitioning, and extend it with networked multiplayer and custom shader effects.
learning_objectives: Implement game architecture patterns including game loops, service locators, and command patterns
Build an Entity-Component-System framework with data-oriented design principles
Implement A* pathfinding on grids and navigation meshes
Apply spatial partitioning structures for efficient collision queries
Write vertex and fragment shaders and implement basic networked state synchronization
covered_materials: Game architecture patterns and fixed-timestep game loops
Entity-Component-System and data-oriented design
Finite and hierarchical state machines
A* pathfinding, heuristics, and navigation meshes
Quadtrees, spatial hashing, and pool allocators
Client-server networking and client-side prediction
Vertex and fragment shader programming

Game Design and Media.title: Level Design
Game Design and Media.description: Study of spatial design principles for game environments, covering player flow, pacing, environmental puzzles, combat arena design, and open world structures. Students graybox levels in Unreal Engine 5, conduct structured playtesting with heat map analysis, and iterate designs based on observed player behavior data.
learning_objectives: Apply level design principles including the teaching triad and visual landmarks
Design pacing structures that alternate tension and relief through spatial composition
Create combat arenas with tactical depth and environmental puzzles with spatial reasoning
Implement procedural generation algorithms and evaluate their design applications
Conduct structured playtesting and iterate designs based on completion funnel data
covered_materials: Level design principles, player flow, and the teaching triad
Pacing, intensity curves, and breathing rooms
Environmental puzzles and combat arena anatomy
Open world vs linear design and hub-and-spoke layouts
Procedural generation: BSP dungeons and Wave Function Collapse
Grayboxing workflow and rapid level prototyping
Playtesting methodology, heat maps, and completion funnels

Game Design and Media.title: Character Design and Development
Game Design and Media.description: Integrates visual character design with personality writing, animation, and companion AI behavior design. Students create production-ready character packages including turnaround sheets, expression studies, personality profiles with motivation triads, FACS-based facial blend shapes, and complete companion AI behavior systems with contextual bark scripts.
learning_objectives: Apply shape language, color psychology, and silhouette principles to character visual design
Construct character personality profiles with motivation triads and distinct character voices
Design facial animation systems using FACS-based blend shapes
Create companion AI behavior systems grounded in established character personalities
Produce production-ready character sheets and design rationale documentation
covered_materials: Character archetypes, shape language, and silhouette design
Character sheets, turnaround views, and production pipeline
Personality profiles, motivation triads, and character voice writing
Character animation principles and FACS facial blend shapes
NPC design, companion AI patterns, and the Elizabeth principle
Contextual bark systems and behavior state machines
Design consistency audits and portfolio preparation

Game Design and Media.title: Game Audio and Sound Design
Game Design and Media.description: Covers the complete game audio pipeline from Foley recording and synthesis through adaptive music systems, spatial audio, and middleware integration. Students create original sound effects, build adaptive music systems in FMOD Studio, implement 3D spatialization with occlusion, and produce a professional demo reel showcasing all audio disciplines.
learning_objectives: Record and process Foley sound effects and design sounds using synthesis techniques
Build adaptive music systems using horizontal re-sequencing and vertical remixing
Implement 3D spatial audio with attenuation, occlusion, and HRTF rendering
Architect game audio projects using FMOD or Wwise middleware
Direct voice acting sessions and implement dialogue priority systems
covered_materials: Audio fundamentals: frequency, amplitude, ADSR, and digital audio formats
Foley recording, subtractive synthesis, and FM synthesis
Adaptive music: horizontal re-sequencing and vertical remixing
Spatial audio, 3D sound, attenuation, and HRTF
Audio middleware: FMOD and Wwise event architecture
Voice acting direction and dialogue management systems
Procedural audio and final mix/mastering techniques

Game Design and Media.title: 3D Game Development
Game Design and Media.description: Advanced 3D game development covering 3D mathematics, the graphics rendering pipeline, shader programming, physics simulation, and visual effects. Students master transformation matrices and quaternions, write vertex and fragment shaders, implement collision detection algorithms, and produce a complete polished 3D game as a capstone project.
learning_objectives: Apply 3D vector operations, matrix transformations, and quaternion rotations
Program vertex and fragment shaders for custom rendering effects
Implement physics simulation with collision detection and rigid body dynamics
Create advanced lighting, PBR materials, and particle effect systems
Develop and optimize a complete 3D game for target platform performance
covered_materials: 3D coordinate systems, vectors, and transformation matrices
Quaternion rotations and transform hierarchies
Graphics rendering pipeline and shader programming (HLSL/GLSL)
Physics simulation, collision detection, and integration methods
Physically-based rendering and global illumination
Particle systems and post-processing effects
Performance optimization and level-of-detail systems

Game Design and Media.title: Mobile Game Development
Game Design and Media.description: Specialized course in developing games for iOS and Android platforms, covering touch input design, mobile GPU optimization, monetization implementation, and app store strategy. Students build mobile-optimized prototypes with 60fps performance targets, integrate in-app purchases with server-side receipt validation, and implement live operations infrastructure.
learning_objectives: Design intuitive touch-based control schemes with multi-touch gesture recognition
Optimize rendering and memory usage for mobile GPU architectures and thermal constraints
Implement in-app purchase flows with server-side receipt validation
Integrate analytics SDKs and apply app store optimization techniques
Architect cross-platform deployments with remote configuration and feature flags
covered_materials: Mobile gaming landscape and platform capabilities
Touch input design, virtual joysticks, and haptic feedback
Mobile performance optimization and texture compression (ASTC, ETC2)
Monetization models, IAP implementation, and receipt validation
App store optimization and launch strategy
Cross-platform architecture and platform abstraction
Live operations, remote configuration, and addressable assets

Game Design and Media.title: Virtual Reality and Augmented Reality
Game Design and Media.description: Covers development for VR and AR platforms including hand tracking, spatial interaction design, locomotion systems, and mixed reality. Students implement hand tracking with gesture recognition, build locomotion systems with comfort settings, create diegetic UI with spatial audio, and develop mixed reality applications with scene understanding and spatial anchors.
learning_objectives: Implement hand tracking with gesture recognition and direct/ray-based interaction
Design VR locomotion systems with evidence-based motion sickness mitigation
Build 3D user interfaces and integrate HRTF-based spatial audio
Develop AR experiences with marker tracking, plane detection, and spatial anchors
Optimize VR applications using foveated rendering and single-pass stereo techniques
covered_materials: VR/AR hardware landscape and stereoscopic rendering
Hand tracking, gesture recognition, and interaction affordances
VR locomotion: teleportation, continuous movement, and comfort techniques
3D UI design, diegetic interfaces, and spatial audio
AR marker tracking, plane detection, and scene mesh occlusion
Spatial anchors and persistent mixed reality content
VR performance optimization and platform deployment

Game Design and Media.title: Game Testing and Quality Assurance
Game Design and Media.description: Professional game QA methodology covering test planning, bug reporting, automated testing, performance profiling, and certification requirements. Students write comprehensive test plans, build automated test suites integrated into CI pipelines, conduct structured playtesting sessions, and prepare games for platform certification submission.
learning_objectives: Write comprehensive test plans and file professional-quality bug reports
Design and execute regression test suites with automated testing frameworks
Profile game performance to identify CPU, GPU, and memory bottlenecks
Conduct structured playtesting and distinguish usability, focus, and balance testing
Prepare games for platform certification across console, PC, and mobile storefronts
covered_materials: QA methodology and the game development lifecycle
Test plan creation, test cases, and bug reporting standards
Regression testing, smoke testing, and CI/CD integration
Performance profiling, frame time analysis, and compatibility testing
Playtesting methodology, user research, and heat map analysis
Platform certification requirements (XR, TRC, Lotcheck)
Save data integrity testing and compliance checklists

Game Design and Media.title: Game Marketing and Publishing
Game Design and Media.description: Covers the complete game marketing lifecycle from pre-announcement through post-launch, including press kit creation, community building, trailer production, influencer outreach, and launch strategy. Students develop a full marketing campaign with professional assets, content calendars, and a detailed launch timeline with contingency planning.
learning_objectives: Create professional press kits with optimized screenshots, key art, and descriptions
Build and manage game communities across Discord, social media, and content platforms
Plan, storyboard, and edit effective game trailers with proper pacing and hooks
Execute influencer outreach campaigns with personalized creator relations
Develop comprehensive launch and post-launch marketing strategies
covered_materials: Game marketing fundamentals and the awareness-to-conversion funnel
Press kit creation and game description writing
Community building and social media content strategy
Game trailer production and video marketing
Influencer relations, key distribution, and embargo strategy
Launch planning, timing, and post-launch roadmap communication
Discount strategy and Steam algorithm optimization

Game Design and Media.title: Multiplayer Game Design
Game Design and Media.description: Covers the design and engineering of networked multiplayer games, from client-server architecture and state synchronization through matchmaking, anti-cheat systems, and live operations infrastructure. Students build a functional multiplayer prototype with authoritative server, client-side prediction, and lag compensation, plus design complete competitive systems.
learning_objectives: Implement client-server architecture with authoritative state management
Apply snapshot interpolation, delta compression, and client-side prediction techniques
Design matchmaking systems using rating algorithms like TrueSkill and Glicko-2
Architect multi-layer anti-cheat systems with server-side validation and statistical detection
Plan server infrastructure with auto-scaling, zero-downtime deployment, and monitoring
covered_materials: Multiplayer network architectures: client-server, peer-to-peer, and hybrid topologies
State synchronization, snapshot interpolation, and delta compression
Latency compensation, client-side prediction, and server reconciliation
Matchmaking systems, rating algorithms, and queue management
Anti-cheat design, server authority, and statistical detection
Live operations, auto-scaling, and incident response
Zero-downtime deployment and monitoring dashboards

Game Design and Media.title: Indie Game Development
Game Design and Media.description: Prepares students to ship commercially viable games as solo developers or small teams, covering scope management, budget art strategies, funding options, early access methodology, and sustainable career planning. Students build a polished vertical slice using rapid prototyping techniques and produce marketing materials suitable for a store page or crowdfunding campaign.
learning_objectives: Apply MoSCoW prioritization and vertical slice methodology to manage project scope
Select and execute art styles achievable within small team constraints
Evaluate funding options including crowdfunding, publishers, grants, and early access
Design early access strategies with structured community feedback loops
Conduct post-mortem analysis and plan sustainable indie development careers
covered_materials: Indie game market landscape and financial realities
Scope management, MoSCoW method, and rapid prototyping
Budget art styles, visual coherence, and post-processing as a force multiplier
Crowdfunding campaigns, publisher deals, and alternative funding
Early access strategy, update cadence, and feedback triage
Post-mortem analysis and common failure patterns
Career sustainability, burnout prevention, and back catalog building

Game Design and Media.title: Game Analytics and Monetization
Game Design and Media.description: Data-driven approach to game design covering player analytics pipelines, retention analysis, A/B testing methodology, and monetization system design. Students analyze real player datasets, design statistically valid experiments, build revenue projection models, and create ethical monetization systems that pass regulatory compliance audits.
learning_objectives: Set up analytics pipelines and track key performance indicators across business models
Build and interpret retention curves, funnel analyses, and cohort comparisons
Design statistically valid A/B tests with proper sample size calculations
Evaluate monetization models and design ethical free-to-play revenue systems
Build revenue projection models and conduct regulatory compliance audits
covered_materials: Analytics pipeline: instrumentation, collection, storage, and dashboards
Retention curves, FTUE optimization, and re-engagement mechanics
A/B testing methodology, statistical significance, and guardrail metrics
Free-to-play monetization: cosmetics, battle passes, and broad monetization
Ethical monetization, loot box regulation, and dark pattern identification
Revenue modeling, LTV calculation, and data-driven design decisions
Data privacy compliance: GDPR, COPPA, and CCPA

Game Design and Media.title: Serious Games and Gamification
Game Design and Media.description: Applies game design principles to solve real-world problems in education, health, training, and social impact. Students analyze gamification frameworks using the Octalysis model and self-determination theory, evaluate FDA-approved digital therapeutics, and build a serious game prototype with embedded assessment and accessibility features.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between serious games, gamification, and game-based learning approaches
Apply the Octalysis framework and self-determination theory to gamification design
Design educational games with scaffolded difficulty and embedded stealth assessment
Evaluate health games and digital therapeutics using clinical evidence criteria
Create accessible serious game prototypes with measurable learning outcomes
covered_materials: Serious games taxonomy, history, and application domains
Gamification frameworks, Octalysis, and behavioral psychology
Educational game design, Bloom's taxonomy, and stealth assessment
Health games, digital therapeutics, and FDA regulatory pathways
Training simulations, Kirkpatrick's evaluation model, and ROI measurement
Social impact games, procedural rhetoric, and persuasive design ethics
Accessibility guidelines and inclusive serious game design

Game Design and Media.title: Game Industry Professional Practices
Game Design and Media.description: Prepares students for game industry careers covering portfolio development, resume writing, interview preparation, Agile project management, intellectual property law, and workplace sustainability. Students build professional portfolios, execute multi-sprint Agile production simulations in teams, and develop career strategies informed by industry research.
learning_objectives: Build discipline-specific portfolios with case studies following problem/process/solution format
Write industry-targeted resumes and prepare for behavioral and technical interviews
Execute Agile/Scrum methodology with sprint planning, retrospectives, and velocity tracking
Distinguish between copyright, trademark, and patent protections as applied to games
Evaluate studio practices around crunch culture and sustainable development
covered_materials: Game industry structure, career paths, and studio organization
Portfolio development, personal branding, and case study presentation
Resume writing, ATS optimization, and interview preparation
Agile and Scrum methodology adapted for game development
Intellectual property law, work-for-hire, and contract negotiation
Workplace ethics, crunch culture, and career sustainability
Salary negotiation and industry compensation data

Game Design and Media.title: Game Design Capstone Project
Game Design and Media.description: Culminating team project taking a game from concept to gold build over 15 weeks through five milestone-driven phases: concept document, playable prototype, alpha, beta, and gold master. Teams manage production using version control and project management tools, conduct external playtesting, and deliver a professional postmortem alongside a portfolio-quality finished game.
learning_objectives: Execute milestone-driven game development from concept through gold master
Validate core mechanics through prototyping and structured external playtesting
Manage scope, risk, and production schedule across a multi-month team project
Apply feature lock discipline, bug triage, and systematic QA processes
Write professional postmortem analysis with honest self-assessment
covered_materials: Team formation, role assignment, and production planning
Concept documents, vision statements, and risk registers
Prototype validation and pivot decision frameworks
Alpha milestone: feature completeness and feature lock
Beta milestone: content completeness, balance tuning, and QA
Gold master criteria, polish budgeting, and regression testing
Postmortem format, portfolio framing, and public presentation

Game Design and Media.title: Game Industry Internship
Game Design and Media.description: Supervised professional experience at an approved game industry site with weekly academic seminars. Students set SMART professional development goals, maintain structured reflection journals, navigate workplace integration, and produce a comprehensive final report with NDA-compliant portfolio additions documenting their professional growth and contributions.
learning_objectives: Set and track SMART professional development goals throughout the internship
Navigate professional studio environments and adapt to industry tools and workflows
Solicit, receive, and act on supervisor feedback for continuous improvement
Document internship contributions in NDA-compliant portfolio case studies
Synthesize the internship experience into a professional career narrative
covered_materials: Internship planning, goal-setting, and communication protocols
Workplace integration, professional communication, and imposter syndrome
Technical skills gaps between academic training and industry practice
Mid-term reflection, goal revision, and supervisor feedback solicitation
Supervisor evaluation, self-evaluation, and comparative analysis
NDA-compliant portfolio documentation and transferable skills inventory
Final presentation, career launch planning, and relationship maintenance

Game Design and Media.title: Programming for Games
Game Design and Media.description: Introductory programming course using C# with a game development focus, progressing from variables and control flow through functions and object-oriented programming to game loop architecture and collision detection. Students build a text-based RPG battle system and a real-time 2D game prototype with keyboard input and AABB collision.
learning_objectives: Declare variables, use data types, and write conditional and loop-based control flow
Design and call functions following the Single Responsibility Principle
Model game entities as classes with encapsulation, constructors, and methods
Implement a fixed-timestep game loop with frame-rate-independent movement
Detect and respond to axis-aligned bounding box collisions between game objects
covered_materials: Variables, primitive data types, and type safety
Conditionals, comparison operators, and logical operators
For loops, while loops, and common loop pitfalls
Functions, parameters, return values, and code organization
Object-oriented programming: classes, objects, and encapsulation
The game loop, delta time, and frame-rate independence
AABB collision detection and event-driven input handling

Game Design and Media.title: Digital Design Principles
Game Design and Media.description: Builds professional digital design skills covering grid-based layouts, typography, color accessibility, branding systems, motion design, responsive layout, and design system architecture. Students create brand identities with responsive landing pages, build scalable component libraries with design tokens, and prototype micro-interactions with specified motion parameters.
learning_objectives: Apply grid systems and visual hierarchy principles to digital layouts
Select and pair typefaces with accessible color palettes meeting WCAG 2.1 AA standards
Construct brand identity systems with style guides and usage rules
Design purposeful micro-interactions with specified duration and easing parameters
Architect scalable design systems using atomic design methodology and design tokens
covered_materials: Grid systems: column grids, modular grids, and responsive adaptation
Typography, typographic scale, and color accessibility (WCAG 2.1)
Branding, identity systems, and style guide construction
Motion design, micro-interactions, and easing curves
Responsive design, mobile-first approach, and auto-layout
Atomic design methodology and component libraries
Design tokens, theming, and design-to-development handoff

Game Design and Media.title: Media Studies
Game Design and Media.description: Examines how media systems operate and how audiences interact with content across platforms, covering media theory, digital platform economics, audience analysis, transmedia storytelling, social media strategy, and media ethics. Students analyze cross-platform media phenomena, build audience personas, and evaluate intellectual property and misinformation issues.
learning_objectives: Apply media theory frameworks including McLuhan, Hall, and Jenkins to contemporary media
Analyze platform economics, algorithmic distribution, and attention economy dynamics
Conduct audience analysis using demographic, psychographic, and behavioral segmentation
Evaluate transmedia storytelling strategies and platform-native content adaptation
Apply ethical frameworks to media production including copyright and fair use analysis
covered_materials: Media theory: McLuhan, Hall's encoding/decoding, and critical media theory
Digital media landscape, platform economics, and algorithmic curation
Audience analysis, psychographic segmentation, and persona development
Transmedia storytelling, world-building, and platform-native content
Social media strategy, content pillars, and community management
Media ethics, copyright, fair use, and Creative Commons
Misinformation, disinformation, and fact-checking methodologies
